Tests the division of a boolean by undefined resulting in NaN.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the result of dividing a boolean value `true` by `undefined`. The expression `true / undefined` evaluates to `NaN` (Not-a-Number) in JavaScript. The `assert` function verifies that the result of this operation is indeed `NaN` by comparing it with `true` using the `isNaN` function. This test ensures that the JavaScript engine correctly handles the division of a boolean by an undefined value, which is a narrow functionality check.
